title: OSMM Performance Measurement Object
description: Measurement-category object (append-only) that records actual performance against a Measurement Framework's plan for a period: the readings, their variance to target, and a narrative readout. The framework is the plan (what will be measured and the targets); this object is the actuals. One object type serves Phase 7 sub-processes 7.1/7.3/7.4/7.5 via a 'dimension' facet (overall / offer / creative / journey / channel / experience / campaign). Canonical schema for object_type 'performance_measurement'. The osmm-performance-measurement-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

performance_measurement_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'PFM-' + lowercase hyphen slug, scoped to the subject plus the period (e.g. 'PFM-wendys-2026-q1'). Each period is a new instance — append-only, never overwritten. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump only to correct a recorded reading; new periods are new instances, not new versions.

name.description: Readable label, usually subject + period (e.g. "Wendy's FY2026 Q1 Performance").

linked_measurement_framework.description: Id of the Measurement Framework this reports against — the plan whose targets these readings are measured against. Use 'MEF-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists. Measurement references what it measures; it never re-defines the metrics.

dimension.description: The lens of this measurement. 'overall' is the strategy-level rollup (7.1); the rest scope the reading to one subject type (7.3 offer, 7.4 creative, 7.5 journey/channel) and fold in the former per-dimension Performance objects.

subject_reference.description: The specific object measured when 'dimension' is not 'overall' — e.g. an Offer (OFR-), Creative Strategy (CRS-), Journey (JNY-), Experience (EXP-), or Campaign Strategy (CMS-). Omit for 'overall'. Use the '<PREFIX>-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' form until the subject exists.

start.description: Period start (ISO date or readable label, e.g. '2026-01-01' or 'FY2026 Q1').

end.description: Period end. Optional for an open or single-label period.

label.description: Human label for the period (e.g. 'FY2026 Q1'). Optional.

metrics.description: The readings — one per KPI measured, reporting the actual against the framework's target. Reference the framework's metric names; do not redefine them.

name.description: Metric name — should match a metric defined in the linked Measurement Framework.

actual.description: The recorded reading for the period (value or banded/directional result; keep public examples honest).

target.description: The target being measured against (echo of the framework's target). Optional.

variance.description: Actual vs. target, directional or quantified (e.g. '+4 pts', 'below plan'). Optional.

status.description: Governed read of the metric vs. its target. Use 'no_target' when the framework set none. Optional.

note.description: Short context on the reading (data caveat, driver). Optional.

summary.description: Narrative readout for the period — what performed and the headline takeaways. Optional.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. Use 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists. Optional.

linked_marketing_strategy.description: Id of the Marketing Strategy whose objectives this period evaluates. Use 'MKS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date.
